prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins on a nearly black screen. In the center there appears to be very faint, low-contrast text that may read “Prime Time,” but it is difficult to make out clearly.

By the final sampled frame, the screen is still black but now displays a centered Oldsmobile end card. A small vertical Oldsmobile emblem appears above the brand name. Beneath it, large white text reads “OLDSMOBILE.” A thin horizontal line separates the brand name from a smaller tagline below: “THE POWER OF INTELLIGENT ENGINEERING.”

No people, vehicles, settings, or actions are visible in the sampled frames. The intervening visual content between the first and last frames is not shown, so any changes during that period cannot be described from the available frames.
